meta.title: The wild F-35 UPGRADES pitched by the designer of the YF-23
meta.description:
Go to https://ground.news/Sandboxx to stay fully informed on military developments around the world. Subscribe through my link right now for 40% off their Vantage Plan, which is what I use everyday.

– BREAK –

Check out Graham Warwick's interview with YF-23 designer Darold Cummings in Aviation Week here: https://aviationweek.com/aerospace/aircraft-propulsion/yf-23-designer-weighs-how-lockheed-martin-could-evolve-f-35

You can find more of Darold Cummings work at ForzAero here:
http://forzaero.net/

According to Lockheed Martin’s CEO, Jim Taiclet, they believe they can port many of the advanced technologies developed for the company’s 6th-generation fighter over to the F-35, making something of a 5th-and-a-half generation franken-fighter that they claim can deliver 80% of a 6th gen-jet’s capabilities for 50% of the price. 

But what exactly might such a fighter look like? Well, fortunately for us, Graham Warwick at Aviation Week recently published an incredible interview with Darold Cummings, the designer of Northrop’s legendary YF-23, breaking down a very logical approach to modifying today’s F-35 for tomorrow’s battlefield, borrowing directly from past Lockheed efforts like the F-16XL and tailless Raptor designs in the X-44 Manta. 

And the results are, well, promising to say the least.
